dublinCore.dc:title: 117 HR 8205 RH: Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Reauthorization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Quigley (for himself and Mr. Calvert) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

Additional sponsors: Mr. Fitzpatrick, Ms. Schakowsky, Mr. McCaul, Mr. Auchincloss, Mr. DesJarlais, Ms. Craig, Mr. Valadao, Mr. Crow, Mr. Thompson of Pennsylvania, Ms. Moore of Wisconsin, Mr. Hurd of Colorado, Mr. Schneider, Mr. Joyce of Ohio, Mr. Veasey, Ms. Salazar, Ms. Bonamici, Mr. Bilirakis, Mr. Sherman, Mr. Garbarino, Mr. Lynch, Mr. Bacon, Mr. Cohen, Mr. Sessions, Mr. Smith of Washington, Mr. Obernolte, Ms. Dean of Pennsylvania, Mr. Cole, Mrs. Foushee, Mr. Rutherford, Ms. Sewell, Mr. Wilson of South Carolina, Mr. Davis of Illinois, Mr. Aderholt, Mr. Torres of New York, Mr. Mann, Ms. Norton, Mr. Van Drew, Mr. Krishnamoorthi, Mr. Alford, Ms. Underwood, Mr. LaLota, Ms. Schrier, Mrs. Kim, Ms. Matsui, Mr. Edwards, Mrs. Dingell, Mr. Walberg, Ms. Tlaib, Mr. Weber of Texas, Mr. Thompson of California, Mr. Bost, Mr. Tonko, Mrs. Bice, Ms. DelBene, Mr. Diaz-Balart, Mr. Correa, Mr. LaHood, Mr. Kennedy of New York, Mr. Womack, Mr. Whitesides, Mr. Rouzer, Mr. Bishop, Ms. Letlow, Ms. Kelly of Illinois, Mr. Amodei of Nevada, Mr. Gottheimer, Mr. Moore of North Carolina, Mr. Cleaver, Mr. Carter of Texas, Mr. Stanton, Mr. Wittman, Mr. Vindman, Mr. Guest, Ms. Bynum, Mrs. Miller of West Virginia, Mrs. Hayes, Mr. Latimer, Mr. Davis of North Carolina, Ms. McBride, Mr. Riley of New York, Mr. Conaway, Ms. Stansbury, Ms. Ross, Ms. Pingree, Mr. Himes, Mrs. Watson Coleman, Mr. Casten, Mr. Landsman, Mrs. Grijalva, Ms. Mejia, Mr. Larson of Connecticut, Mr. Sorensen, Mr. Peters, Mr. Jackson of Illinois, Ms. Adams, Ms. Budzinski, Mrs. Beatty, Mr. Magaziner, Ms. Kaptur, Mr. Carter of Louisiana, Mr. Suozzi, and Ms. Scanlon
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on April 6, 2026

form.official-title: To amend the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act to reauthorize the provisions of such Act through fiscal year 2031, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Reauthorization Act of 2026.

section.header: Reauthorization of Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act

subsection.text: Section 7 of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act (Public Law 117–79) is amended by striking 2026 and inserting 2031.
Section 2(f) of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act (21 U.S.C. 360ee note) is amended by striking 2026 and inserting 2031.

section.header: Improvements to program for grants for research on therapies for ALS

subsection.header: Renewal of grants for research on therapies for ALS review
subsection.text: Section 2(b) of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act (21 U.S.C. 360ee note) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.header: Renewal of grants for research on therapies for ALS review
paragraph.text: In reviewing applications for renewals of a grant awarded under this section with respect to an investigational drug, the Secretary shall request from the manufacturer or sponsor, and assess, the enrollment, safety, and any available efficacy data relating to the investigational drug in the prevention, diagnosis, mitigation, treatment, or cure of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

subsection.text: Section 2(c) of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act (21 U.S.C. 360ee note) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2)(B), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (3), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

the entity seeking such grant will promptly report any new and serious adverse events and safety information that is considered to be unexpected with respect to the phase 3 trial to the grant-making institution, in addition to complying with the safety reporting requirements under section 312.32 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations).

subsection.text: Section 2(e) of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act (21 U.S.C. 360ee note) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: The term phase 3, with respect to a clinical trial, includes a phase 2/3 combined trial that begins enrollment within a timeframe, determined by the Secretary through the terms and conditions of the grant awarded under this section.

section.header: FDA rare neurodegenerative disease action plan
section.text: Section 4 of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Act (21 U.S.C. 360aa note) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the section heading, by striking ALS and other rare neurodegenerative disease action plan and inserting FDA rare neurodegenerative disease action plan; and
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: FDA rare neurodegenerative disease action plan

paragraph.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Reauthorization Act of 2026, the Commissioner of Food and Drugs shall publish on the website of the Food and Drug Administration an action plan that includes a description of the actions that the Food and Drug Administration intends to take during the 5-year period following publication of the action plan with respect to the program enhancements, policy development, regulatory science initiatives, and other appropriate initiatives described in subsection (a).

Not later than 5 years after the date of enactment of the Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Reauthorization Act of 2026, the Commissioner of Food and Drugs shall publish on the website of the Food and Drug Administration a report that describes the actions taken by the Food and Drug Administration under the action plan published under paragraph (1) and the extent to which such action plan meets the requirements specified in paragraph (1).

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
